title: Fluffy Roasted Egyptian Lentil Tagine
description: A hearty and flavorful Egyptian dish made with roasted lentils and a blend of aromatic spices, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe uses a terracotta dish and bicarbonate of soda to create a fluffy texture.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Rinse the lentils and soak them in water for 30 minutes.
Drain the lentils and mix with bicarbonate of soda, olive oil, onion, garlic, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper in a terracotta dish.
Cover the dish with a lid and roast in the oven for 30 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue roasting for an additional 10 minutes, or until the lentils are fluffy and lightly browned.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Use a fork to fluff the lentils and create a fluffy texture.
Serve the fluffy roasted Egyptian lentil tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
